info.name: BOLO Backend (Django) — Phase 2
info.description:
Covers exactly what's built so far in bolo-backend-django: Auth (OTP + JWT access/refresh), minimal Labels, and the core Task lifecycle. Does NOT cover subtasks/comments/evidence/voice-recording/full label CRUD/broadcasts -- those aren't built yet (Phase 3+).

Setup, once:
1. `source .venv/bin/activate`
2. `python manage.py migrate --settings=config.settings.dev`
3. `python manage.py seed_dev_data --settings=config.settings.dev` (idempotent -- creates a fixed tenant + assigner/assignee user, IDs already baked into this collection's variables)
4. `python manage.py runserver --settings=config.settings.dev`

Auth notes:
- This API is httpOnly-cookie based, not header/Bearer-token based. Use the Postman **desktop app** (not the browser/web version) so cookies persist automatically between requests -- no manual token copying needed.
- OTP codes aren't emailed anywhere real in dev -- they print to the `runserver` terminal (console email backend). Copy the 6-digit code from there into the `otp` collection variable before running Verify OTP.
- Only one user can be "logged in" at a time in Postman's shared cookie jar (same domain = same cookies, just like a browser). To act as the other role (e.g. switch from assigner to assignee), re-run that user's Request OTP + Verify OTP pair -- it overwrites the session.
- Task/label IDs are captured automatically into collection variables by the test scripts on Create Task / Create Label, so you don't need to copy those by hand.

request.description: Public route. Sends a 6-digit OTP -- in dev it prints to the runserver console (no real email sent). Copy the code into the `otp` variable, then run Verify OTP (Assigner).

Public route. On success sets two httpOnly cookies (token = 15-min access JWT, refresh_token = 7-day rotating refresh token) -- Postman stores these automatically and sends them on every subsequent request to this host. This logs you in AS THE ASSIGNER for all following requests until you verify-otp as someone else.
Same as above, but for the assignee user. Use this + Verify OTP (Assignee) whenever you need to act as the assignee (accept, done-a).
Logs you in AS THE ASSIGNEE, overwriting the assigner's session cookie (only one active session per browser/Postman cookie jar, same as production).

Uses the refresh_token cookie (not the access token) to mint a new access+refresh pair. Rotates the refresh token -- the old one becomes unusable immediately (replaying it, or any already-used refresh token, returns 401 and revokes every refresh token for that user).
Requires an authenticated session (access token cookie). Clears both cookies server-side and revokes the current refresh token.
Requires login (any user). Label names are unique per creator, not per tenant -- creating the same name twice as the same user returns 409 CONFLICT.
Returns only labels created by the currently logged-in user.
Same underlying query as /labels/mine/ -- both surface the caller's own labels, just for different UI pickers (main-label vs personal-label).

Run as the assigner. mainLabelId is optional -- remove it (or leave {{labelId}} empty) if you haven't created a label yet. Omit dueDate entirely to create a DRAFT task instead of OPEN (no notification fires for a DRAFT).
Run as the assigner. `view` is required: assigned | delegated | needs_attention. Optional: labelId, isArchived, page, limit.
Run as the assignee to see this task in their inbox.
Dashboard tab counts for whichever user is currently logged in: {assigned, delegated, needsAttention}.

Caller must be the assigner or assignee of this task (403 otherwise, 404 if it doesn't exist in your tenant). subtasks/comments/evidence/voiceRecording will always be empty -- those features aren't built yet.

Assigner-only. All fields optional: assigneeId, dueDate, priority, mainLabelId, description. Sending "title" here returns 400 TITLE_IMMUTABLE -- title can never be changed after creation. Setting dueDate to null on an OPEN task reverts it to DRAFT.
Assignee-only. Requires the task to currently be OPEN -- switch to the assignee's session first (Verify OTP (Assignee)).
Assignee-only. Requires IN_PROGRESS (i.e. already accepted). Marks the assignee's side of the two-step completion.

Assigner-only. Requires DONE_A (or IN_PROGRESS/OVERDUE) and all subtasks DONE_D (vacuously true -- subtasks aren't built yet). Archives the task -- this is terminal, it can no longer be edited, cancelled, or deleted (409 TASK_TERMINAL).
Assigner-only. Works on any non-DONE_D task. Try this on a fresh task (create one first) rather than one you've already run through done-d.
Assigner-only. Requires OPEN/IN_PROGRESS/OVERDUE. Fires an in-app Notification AND an email (printed to the runserver console via the dev email backend).
Assigner-only. Hard delete. Returns 409 TASK_TERMINAL if the task is already DONE_D -- test this on a fresh (non-archived) task.
